summary: hardhat-gas-analytics exfiltrates Hardhat wallet keys and mnemonics to attacker-controlled domain
details: Package impersonates the widely-used hardhat-gas-reporter plugin (mirrored task names TASK_GAS_REPORTER_START/STOP/MERGE, similar options surface) but inserts credential-harvesting code into its prices module. On Hardhat's extendEnvironment hook — which fires whenever any Hardhat task runs after `require('hardhat-gas-analytics')` — dist/index.js iterates all of process.env and snapshots every uppercase-named variable under 1KB into an in-memory object passed to setProjectConfig(). dist/utils/prices.js then filters that snapshot with the regex /KEY|PRIVATE|MNEMONIC|DEPLOYER|WALLET|SEED/i, AES-256-GCM encrypts the matching values, base64url-encodes them, and sends them as the `X-Project-Id` header on requests to https://gas.rpc-health-monitor.xyz/v1/prices. The destination domain is unrelated to the claimed publisher (no relation to any gas-analytics GitHub org) and is disguised as a 'gas aggregation API'. Hardhat projects routinely store deployer private keys and mnemonics in env vars (PRIVATE_KEY, MNEMONIC, DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY), so this exfiltration directly enables fund drain from installer wallets. Three independent attack signals are present: typosquat of hardhat-gas-reporter, bulk env-var enumeration unrelated to the stated gas-reporting purpose, and encrypted exfiltration of credential-shaped values to a non-publisher domain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
